title: oxalis caprina var. abyssinica
intro: Oxalis caprina var. abyssinica, also known as the Abyssinian wood-sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in various herbal remedies due to its medicinal properties, particularly its high oxalic acid content, which has been found to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Oxalis caprina var. abyssinica include its potential use in treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and acting as an antiseptic. It can be prepared as a tea or tincture for consumption, and its leaves and stems can also be used in salves and topical applications to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
